bids.reasoning: It is the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0), and the opponent's starting offer yields minimal utility for me across all issues. To establish a strong anchor and protect my high-value issues, I will counter with my optimal offer. There is no need to make concessions this early in the process.

The opponent repeated their minimum-utility offer (valueF for all issues) at t=0.02. Since it is extremely early in the negotiation, I will make a very small concession on my lowest-weighted issues (issueC and issueD) to signal movement while remaining resolute on the higher-weighted issues (issueA and issueB). This maintains my total utility well above 0.95.

The negotiation is still in its very early stages (t=0.04). The opponent has repeatedly offered their most favorable terms (valueF for all issues) without making any concessions. To encourage movement while protecting my most important issues, I will hold firm on issueA and issueB (where my importance is highest) and make a minor concession on issueD, my least important issue, lowering it to valueC. This offer retains an excellent utility of over 0.93 for me while demonstrating a willingness to negotiate.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening bid of valueF across all issues. Since it is very early in the negotiation (t=0.06), I will make a minor concession on my lowest-weight issues to probe for their preferences while maintaining a utility above 0.90. I am keeping my highest-importance issues (issueA and issueB) at their top values to anchor the negotiation.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.08). The opponent has repeatedly offered their most extreme bid ("valueF" for all issues), showing no willingness to negotiate so far. Since it's still early, I will make a small concession on my lowest-utility issues (issueC and issueD) to demonstrate a willingness to compromise, while continuing to hold firm on my most important issues (issueA and issueB).

The opponent continues to hold firm on their opening offer, offering me my worst possible values on all issues. Given it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.10), I will concede only slightly. I will maintain my top choice for my most important issue (issueA) and make a minor concession on issueB, while keeping my previous position on issueC and issueD, aiming for a utility of 0.84.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their starting offer of valueF for all issues across 6 rounds. Since time is still very early (t=0.12), I will not make large concessions. However, to keep the negotiation moving and signal ongoing flexibility, I am making a minimal concession on my least important issue (issueD), shifting from valueD to valueE.

The negotiation is still early (t=0.14) but the opponent is refusing to concede anything, repeating their opening offer which targets my lowest-utility values (valueF for every issue). Since issueD has the lowest priority for me (weight 0.1) and they are asking for valueF, I will concede completely on issueD to valueF in order to break the deadlock, while firmly maintaining high-utility values on my most important issues (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB). This brings my utility to roughly 0.808, a fair early-game offer showing significant good faith.

The opponent is maintaining a completely inflexible stance, repeatedly offering the minimum possible utility for me across all issues. As it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.16), I am not pressured to make large concessions. I will reorganize my concessions on the less critical issues (issueC and issueD) to present a new combination while keeping my expected utility high and signaling that I will not concede drastically without reciprocation.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on all issues, consistently offering the lowest possible values across the board. Since early the time fraction is only 0.18, I will not concede much further but will instead propose a different configuration of values that yields identical utility to my previous offer. By swapping my stance slightly between issueA and issueB, I can probe for tradeoffs without losing ground.

The opponent has repeatedly sent the exact same minimum-utility offer (valueF for all issues) for the entirety of the negotiation (t=0.00 to t=0.20) and has not shown any willingness to compromise. Because we are still in the early phase (t=0.2), I will not concede heavily but rather offer a slightly different combination of mid-to-high utility constraints to search for an acceptable compromise. I will propose a bid with an expected high utility for myself (~0.85) by slightly conceding on my high-priority issues (issueA and issueB) and requesting top values for lower priorities.

The opponent continues to stubbornly propose the same offer of valueF across all issues, leaving me with their worst utility possible. Assuming a relatively early time of t=0.22, I will keep slowly exploring combinations of mid-to-high utility for me (around 0.80-0.85) to signal a willingness to make concessions, primarily by yielding on lower-weight issues like issueC and issueD, without compromising entirely on my top two priorities.

The opponent is holding extremely firm and has repeated the exact same opening offer at every turn. Since the time fraction is still at an early 0.24, I do not need to concede dramatically, but I do want to show flexibility. I will offer a combination that maintains a very high utility for me by keeping high-importance issues (issueA, issueB, issueC) near my top preferences, while giving the opponent their preferred value (valueF) on my least-important issue (issueD).

The opponent's offers have been completely rigid at their maximum value, offering me the lowest possible utility (0.20). Time pressure is still relatively low at `t=0.26`. I will maintain a firm stance on my high-weight issues (issueA and issueB) while continuing to explore varying combinations on lower-weight issues like issueC and issueD, offering the opponent's preferred 'valueF' on issueD to see if it elicits any flexibility.

The opponent continues to hold firm on their opening bid, showing no flexibility and repeating the exact same low-utility offer across all turns up to t=0.28. Given the lack of movement, I will continue to explore different permutations of mid-level concessions on lower-weight issues (issueC and issueD) while maintaining high utility on my main priorities (issueA and issueB), aiming to stay above an 0.82 utility threshold to encourage reciprocity without overly conceding.

The opponent is remaining completely inflexible, offering their likely ideal value (valueF across all issues) repeatedly without any concession. Given that the time fraction is only 0.3, I will maintain my high-utility targets but continue to alter my composition to explore where they might have some flexibility. I will propose an offer that gives me a utility around 0.85 by holding firm on my most important issue (issueA) and conceding slightly on my less important ones (issueC and issueD).

The opponent continues to stubbornly bid their opening offer (`valueF` for all issues) without making a single concession, despite passing the 30% time mark. Since they are refusing to negotiate in good faith, I will hold relatively firm on my highest-priority issues (`issueA` and `issueB`) to avoid rewarding their stonewalling. I am making only a very minor adjustment to my bid to keep the negotiation open while waiting for the opponent to show some willingness to compromise.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their starting offer (all `valueF`), which yields minimal utility for me, showing extreme obstinacy. Since we are still in the early-to-mid stages of negotiation (t=0.34), I will continue to make measured concessions to explore the issue pairings they might prefer, focusing on preserving utility on my most heavily weighted issues (`issueA` and `issueB`) while trying diverse tradeoffs on `issueC` and `issueD`. My aim is to locate an area of mutual gain without conceding too much utility upfront.

The opponent has maintained a completely rigid posture, asking for 'valueF' across all issues in every single offer so far. To test their flexibility and seek a compromise, I will offer them their preferred 'valueF' on my least important issue (issueD) and concede to 'valueD' on issueC. Meanwhile, I will hold firm on my highest priority issues (issueA and issueB), maintaining a strong utility of ~0.82 for myself during this early-to-mid negotiation phase.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on all issues, continually offering their ideal values of 'valueF'. Since the time fraction is 0.38, we are still moderately early but should show movement on less important issues to prevent a deadlock. I will offer 'valueF' on my lowest importance issue (issueD) and 'valueE' on my second lowest (issueC) to make an attractive concession, while holding strictly to 'valueA' on my most heavily weighted issues (issueA and issueB) to maintain a relatively high utility (0.79).

The opponent continues to stubbornly bid 'valueF' across all issues, offering zero concessions despite my consecutive flexible proposals. Since we are approaching the mid-game at t=0.40, I will aim for a utility around 0.78 by keeping my highest-importance issues (A and B) at highly preferred values, while making a major concession to the opponent's desired 'valueF' on my lowest-importance issue (D) in hopes of prompting some reciprocal movement from them on the major issues.

The opponent is stonewalling, having proposed the exact same offer (valueF for all issues) from the start up to t=0.42. I am keeping my utility target around 0.75 by holding firm on my most important issue (issueA) and conceding slightly on my second most important issue (issueB) and heavily on my least important issues (issueC and issueD) to test if this prompts any departure from the opponent's strategy.

The opponent has rigidly repeated the exact same offer (giving me my minimum possible utility) since the beginning of the negotiation without any variation or messages. At t=0.44, I am continuing to search for an acceptable compromise by systematically offering different value combinations that maintain a sturdy utility for myself (around 0.76) while giving the opponent valueF on the least important issues. Since issueA and issueB are my highest priorities, I will hold mostly firm on those while conceding on issueC and issueD.

The opponent is holding entirely firm, repeatedly bidding 'valueF' for all issues without any concessions or messages. In response, I will concede fully on my two least important issues (issueC and issueD), matching their preferred 'valueF', while holding relatively firm on my highest-weight issues (issueA and issueB). This maintains my utility around 0.71 while exploring if they will accept a compromise heavily weighted towards their preferences on half the board.

The opponent has repeated the exact same offer (all valueF) without making a single concession since the start. Approaching the halfway point, I want to show flexibility while holding onto significant utility, so I will offer the opponent's requested value (valueF) on my less important issues (issueC and issueD). Meanwhile, I'll slightly shift my demands between my heavily weighted issues (issueA and issueB) to probe for any underlying tradeoffs without collapsing my net utility.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on all issues, persistently demanding valueF for everything. Since we are halfway through the negotiation (t=0.5), I will attempt to break the deadlock by yielding on my lower-priority issues. I am offering valueF on issueD and valueE on issueC, while keeping my high-priority issues closer to my ideal to maintain an acceptable overall utility.

The opponent has remained incredibly stubborn, only recently making a minimal concession on my most critical issue (issueA transitioning from valueF to valueE). Given the time fraction is just over the halfway mark (t=0.52), I will maintain high utility on my prioritized issues (issueA and issueB), but explore combinations in the mid-range for issueC and issueD to draw out further movement. The target utility here remains relatively firm, around 0.74.

The opponent has finally shown a slight concession on issueA, moving from valueF to valueE. Since we are just past the midpoint (t=0.54) and they are conceding very slowly, I will maintain high utilities on my most important issues (issueA and issueB), while continuing to offer substantial concessions on my lower-weight issues (issueC and issueD) to find a mutually acceptable package. I am targeting a utility of around 0.75 this turn.

The opponent has rigidly held to valueF for most of the negotiation but recently made a small concession on issueA. This signals that issueA may be of lower importance to them, which aligns perfectly with my preferences since issueA is my most heavily weighted issue. Therefore, I will firmly hold my demand for issueA at valueA while making moderate concessions on lower-weighted issues like issueC and issueD, offering a new combination to find acceptable tradeoffs while maintaining a strong utility (around 0.77).

The opponent is holding very firm overall but recently made a minimal concession on issueA (moving from valueF to valueE). Since issueA and issueB are my most important issues (combined 70% of my utility), I will protect my values there. I can concede further on my lower-weight issues (issueC and issueD) by giving the opponent their preferred valueF, targeting an overall utility around 0.65 to encourage more movement from their side.

The opponent is holding extremely firm on `valueF` for all issues, with only a temporary slight concession to `valueE` on `issueA`. Since `time_fraction` is 0.6, I need to start exploring new combinations that could satisfy their rigid demands while protecting my higher-weighted issues. I will keep my most important issue (`issueA`) at my best value, give `valueF` on my lowest-weighted issue (`issueD`), and offer mild concessions on `issueB` and `issueC`.

Our opponent remains staunchly anchored around valueF for all issues, only occasionally exploring valueE for issues A or B. Given we are at time fraction 0.62, I will make a decisive concession on the issues I care about least (issueC and issueD), offering them the opponent's apparently preferred valueF. In exchange, I will hold firm on issueA (my most critical issue) and offer a moderate concession on issueB to maintain my utility at a reasonable level around 0.66.

The opponent is starting to concede on `issueA` and `issueB` (recently offering `issueA: valueD` and `issueB: valueE`), which indicates these issues might be relatively less important to them than `issueC` and `issueD`, where they are holding firmly at `valueF`. Considering the time fraction (t=0.64), I will capitalize on this by strongly retaining high utility on my highest priority issues (`issueA` and `issueB`) while fully conceding `issueC` and `issueD` to `valueF` to explore an agreement zone around ~0.65 utility.

The opponent is holding extremely firm on issues C and D (consistently bidding `valueF`), while showing minimal concessions on issues A and B interchangeably. Given that I have already conceded entirely on their strong preferences for C and D in my recent offers, I will now test their flexibility on issue B by stepping down slightly to `valueC`, keeping issue A at `valueB` to maintain a moderate utility above 0.60 for myself as time crosses the halfway mark.

The negotiation is progressing past the midpoint (t=0.68). The opponent strongly prefers 'valueF' across all issues, but has occasionally compromised to 'valueD'/'valueE' on issues A and B. Since my weights for issueC and issueD are lowest, I will concede completely to 'valueF' on those to accommodate their preferences. Meanwhile, I will propose a slight concession on my higher-weight issues, aiming for a utility of around 0.58, to find a middle ground without dropping too close to my reservation point.

The opponent has consistently offered `valueF` for all issues, with only very marginal concessions to `valueE` or `valueD` on `issueA` and `issueB`. Since my weights for `issueC` and `issueD` are the lowest, I will continue to fully yield to their preference of `valueF` on these issues to facilitate an agreement. In exchange, I am shifting the balance of my demands towards `issueB` while accepting a moderate concession on `issueA`, targeting a solid overall utility above 0.6.

The opponent remains very stubborn, insisting on valueF for nearly all issues, though they occasionally make minor concessions on issueA and issueB. Since my weights for issueC and issueD are the lowest, I will continue to offer highly favorable values (valueE and valueF) for these issues to meet their apparent priorities. I will keep my highly weighted issues (issueA and issueB) at valueB to maintain a utility of around 0.67, ensuring a reasonable outcome without giving in completely too early.

The opponent has just made a substantial concession on issueA (proposing valueC), which is my most important issue. Since time is at 0.74, I will adopt valueC for issueA to reward this concession while ensuring I compensate by asking for high-utility values on my secondary issues. I am aiming for a utility of around 0.66 by dropping to valueB on issueB and conceding gracefully to valueD on issueC and valueE on issueD.

The opponent has shown consistent demand for `valueF` across most issues but recently conceded on `issueA` up to `valueC`, indicating some flexibility. Since we are at `t=0.76`, I will aim for a utility around 0.65 by matching some of their preferences on `issueC` and `issueD` while securing moderate to high values on my most heavily weighted issues, `issueA` and `issueB`. I will propose a slight concession on my lower-weighted issues to encourage agreement without dropping my utility too drastically.

The negotiation is entering the late stage (t=0.78). The opponent has shown they are willing to concede slightly on issueA (proposing valueC at t=0.76) and issueD (proposing valueE at t=0.78). I will match their concessions by offering exactly valueC for issueA, valueE for issueD, and valueF for issueC (which they have consistently requested). To protect my own utility, I will hold firm on issueB with valueA, resulting in an offer that significantly crosses into their proposed territory while keeping my utility near 0.65.

The opponent has made a significant concession on my most valuable issue (A), offering valueB. With time at 0.8, I want to reinforce this behavior by accepting valueB for issueA, while simultaneously conceding on issueB (moving down to valueC) and keeping issueC at their strong preference of valueF. This maintains my overall utility above 0.60 while closely bridging the gap toward the opponent's offers.

The negotiation is entering the late stage (t=0.82) and the opponent is heavily anchored on `valueF` for all issues. However, they have shown brief willingness to concede on `issueA` up to `valueB` and on `issueB` up to `valueE`. I will fully concede my lowest-weight issues (`issueC` and `issueD`) to their apparent favorite `valueF`, while maintaining a moderate utility for myself on `issueB` (`valueC`) and `issueA` (`valueB`) to propose a realistic compromise before the deadline.

The time fraction is 0.84, so the deadline is approaching. The opponent has repeatedly offered valueF on mostly all issues, but recently conceded to valueB on issueA and valueE on issueB (at t=0.80). I will secure valueF for them on issues C and D where my importance is lowest, and in exchange, request valueB for my most important issue (A), while conceding from valueC to valueD on my second most important issue (B), aiming for a mutually acceptable compromise with roughly 0.55 utility for myself.

The opponent has recently conceded to `valueB` on `issueA`, `valueE` on `issueB` (at t=0.80), and `valueE` on `issueC` (at t=0.86). Since my last offer yielded a utility of 0.552, I can form an offer with a utility of ~0.53 by combining their previously proposed concessions on issues A, B, and C. This constitutes a steady sequence of concessions on my part while relying on values they have shown a willingness to accept.
